kill_fact: No single fact kills the BEAR conclusion (the debate already landed at C / conviction 2 — a pass on scale-out alpha — and the AI/data-center overlay is correctly judged priced-in and commodity-grade). But two LOAD-BEARING balance-sheet/FCF anchors used across all three SoP cases are STALE and materially understate risk, strengthening the bear: (1) NET DEBT IS WRONG. The debate hard-codes '~$250M net debt, <0.7x' into the bull, base AND bear equity bridges. Per the Q2 FY2026 10-Q (period ended 2026-03-27), net debt is actually $318.3M ($760.6M total debt less $442.3M cash) — already $68M worse than the anchor and ~0.9x the $350M trough EBITDA. (2) HIDDEN CASH DRAIN NOT IN THE NUMBERS. Atkore settled PVC-pipe antitrust claims for $136.5M (DPP $72.5M + NCSP $64M, agreed 2026-04-28, 'available cash on-hand,' booked as non-operating expense in Q2) AND agreed a FURTHER $50M End User settlement on 2026-06-03 — ~$186.5M of real cash outflow, ~63% of one year's FCF (~$295.7M). The $50M June leg post-dates the 3/27 balance sheet, so reported net debt marches toward ~$368M (~1.05x trough EBITDA) once funded. The debate dossier mentions only a $136.5M 'charge' and explicitly asserts the settlement does NOT impair the FCF floor or net-debt anchor — that is false; the FCF-yield value pitch and the 'fortress <0.7x' claim are both overstated.
corrections: Replace 'net debt ~$250M, <0.7x' with the actual Q2 FY2026 figure: net debt $318.3M (~0.9x trough EBITDA) as of 2026-03-27, trending to ~$368M (~1.05x) after the 2026-06-03 $50M End User settlement is funded. Haircut the FCF-floor: ~$186.5M cumulative PVC-antitrust cash outflow (April $136.5M + June $50M) consumes ~63% of FY2025 FCF (~$295.7M), so the headline ~10.8% FCF yield overstates distributable/owner cash this cycle. Cash on hand is $442.3M (Q2 FY2026), not the $506.7M FY2025 figure cited in the dossier. The other load-bearing facts VERIFY against primary 8-K/10-Q sources: FY2025 adj EBITDA $386.4M (vs FY2024 $771.7M), FY2025 FCF $295.7M, FY2026 guide adj EBITDA $340-360M / net sales $2.9-2.95B / adj EPS $5.05-5.55, Q2 FY2026 rev $731.4M and adj EPS $1.23 (beat-and-maintain), ~5% organic volume growth. Data-center demand is confirmed real but UNQUANTIFIED in the filings (qualitative 'ramp in 2H 2026'), supporting the moderator's 'priced-in, commodity slice, no hidden AI winner' read.
evidence: Atkore Q2 FY2026 10-Q (period ended 2026-03-27), SEC EDGAR: cash & equivalents $442.3M, total debt $760.6M, net debt $318.3M (~0.9x trough EBITDA) — contradicts the debate's '~$250M, <0.7x' anchor. https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001666138/000162828026030764/atkr-20260327.htm

Atkore 8-K, PVC-pipe antitrust settlements executed 2026-04-28: $136.5M aggregate (DPP $72.5M + NCSP $64M), funded from 'available cash on-hand,' booked as non-operating expense in the quarter ended 2026-03-27. https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/ATKR/8-k-atkore-inc-reports-material-event-42396bb2aa3a.html

Atkore additional PVC End User settlement agreed 2026-06-03 for $50M (separate from the April $136.5M), per Law360 / open class-action filings — brings cumulative PVC-antitrust cash outflow to ~$186.5M (~63% of FY2025 FCF). https://www.law360.com/amp/articles/2471475

Atkore Q2 FY2026 earnings 8-K (Ex 99.1, 2026-05-05): rev $731.4M, adj EPS $1.23 (beat), FY2026 guide adj EBITDA $340-360M / sales $2.9-2.95B / adj EPS $5.05-5.55 maintained; FY2025 adj EBITDA $386.4M vs FY2024 $771.7M, FY2025 FCF $295.7M — core operating facts verify. https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001666138/000162828026030054/atkr2q26exhibit991.htm
